---
title: "The Sensor Footprint Function"
date: 2026-03-22
description: "How satellite observations are modeled as weighted averages 
of an underlying spatial field, and why the weighting function matters"
categories: [spatial statistics, remote sensing, downscaling]
---
  
  ## The problem
  
  Satellite sensors don't measure a single point — they measure a blurry 
weighted average over a region of the earth's surface. The shape of that 
blur matters.

## The idea

Each observation $y_i$ is modeled as:
  
  $$y_i = \frac{1}{|D_i|}\int_{D_i} g_i(s) v(s) \, ds + \varepsilon_i$$
  
  where $g_i(s)$ is the footprint response function — for OCO-2, 
approximately a super-Gaussian describing the sensor's sensitivity 
across its footprint.

## In SpatialBasis

[How the package handles footprint specification]

## Example

[Code example]
